description: Issuing one query per row of a result set (often via an ORM lazy load) instead of a single set-based join or batch.
body: Failure mode: code fetches a list then loops issuing a query per element (the N+1 pattern), so 1 + N round trips hammer the database and latency scales with row count. Detection signals: query logs show the same parameterized statement repeated N times; latency grows linearly with result size; an ORM emits SELECTs inside a loop. Do not lazy-load per row in a loop. Safer fallback: replace with a single JOIN or a WHERE id IN (...) batch, use the ORM's eager-load/join-fetch, and verify the query log shows a constant small number of statements regardless of N.
